reviews.review_description: I really like this game so far. I’ve only had it for a couple days and its addictive. Besides earning money to unlock new recipes and upgrade appliances, I don’t see any other goals to work towards which would make it more fun and rewarding.  Or maybe I just haven’t played enough yet to see them. Otherwise cool game for the on sale price

I love this type of game and this game is clearly a labour of love from the dev! I really like how free and realistic this game is; I’m not locked behind a counter and have to actually use genuine work skills to keep up with serving tables, collecting dirty plates etc. I only dropped a star because there were a couple of mechanics that felt “clunky” - I struggle to pick up the tray with plate and cup, the light up outline is really helpful, but I still found selecting it difficult and a bit frustrating. The tongs respawning away from the microwave was a little annoying. Overall the game is great as it is, but looking forward to seeing it developed and updated, purely because it is lots of fun seeing what is possible!

Amazing game i love relaxing games like this even though i sweet sometimes bc im grinding. But otherwise great game amazing.
btw

reviews.review_title: My thoughts about a multiplayer game
reviews.review_description: So if they make a multiplayer game like coffee quest I think a lot more people would buy this game

I was genuinely surprised by how realistic the simulation feels. The recipes and tutorials are extremely well done — they break everything down into clear stages, which makes learning each drink surprisingly easy and enjoyable. There’s loads to do as well: hoovering the floor, waiting on tables, washing dishes, choosing the correct crockery… it’s a full shift!

I found it immensely good fun, but it also gave me a real appreciation for how stressful coffee shop work can be in real life when customers are left waiting too long. A brilliant, engaging experience overall.

Tutorial improvements are in the works! Join our Discord at https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh for updates and to connect with other players! Happy brewing! 🎮

reviews.review_description: bro it looks so cool but i just got this game

I can't believe how much fun this is - and it's so challenging, I had to switch to casual mode for practice! I feel like a kid again, playing make believe with my toy foods and dishes, and I have enjoyed many hours doing this!  I had a problem with using the whisk to make Matcha in the tutorial, but the developers fixed this bug as soon as I reported it - they are great!  I look forward to seeing more improvements in the future.  I would like to buy some more cups, different foods, and maybe other upgrades for the place.  It would be cool to interact with customers in some way (maybe waving to acknowledge their presence and show friendly customer service for a better tip or review). An employee would be nice.  Multiplayer with private rooms would be fantastic - I'd gift the game to a friend right now if that were an option!  One slight glitch:  I play on a Quest 2 and have been kicked out a couple of times with a message from Meta saying there was not enough memory to run the game, but when I went back in, it worked fine, and my earnings and purchases had been saved. I highly recommend this game!

reviews.review_description: as a chef and a gamer i can confidently say i love this game. definitely stream youtube music in the background for the real feel
Amazing game its so fun

Coffee Quest VR takes the cooking-sim formula to the next level by focusing on one of the world’s favorite drinks: coffee. The variety of tasks is impressive—you’re not just pulling shots, but also clearing tables, washing plates, vacuuming, emptying rubbish, and even hiring a band to liven up your café.

The game does a fantastic job of showing the balance a barista owner must strike: staying organized, handling pressure, and juggling multiple responsibilities while keeping customers happy. Learning and perfecting the different coffee styles is both fun and challenging—especially when the orders start piling up.

With its blend of realism, variety, and pressure-filled chaos, Coffee Quest VR is one of the most advanced and engaging café simulations out there. Easily a 5-star experience!

developer_response.body: Thank you for capturing the spirit of the game so well. I love crafting that balance of organization, pressure, and satisfaction, and I’m working on more depth and quality-of-life updates. To keep up with new releases and offer suggestions, please join the Discord: https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh

reviews.review_description: This game is super fun, I love all the recipes and different foods people can order, but I am BEGGING the developers to please add a casual mode 🙏🏼 I love playing the game, but it gets way stressful and you have to close your shop sometimes to fill all the orders, clean, wash dishes, order food, etc. There’s just no way to get it all done. The coffee orders take time and customers get mad and leave. I would love a casual mode similar to Clash of Chefs where there are not time limits and you can just make items and have fun. I’d love to see that implemented in a future update.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the thoughtful review! I’ve added a true Casual Mode with no time limits so you can make drinks and experiment at your own pace. I also redesigned the entire café to improve flow, visibility, and how cleanup fits into the rhythm. If you try the new mode, I’d love to hear how it feels and what would make it even cozier. You can stay up to date and share feedback here: https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh

reviews.review_title: Add clanking sounds when you hit pitcher
reviews.review_description:
Add clanking sounds when you hit the pitcher or portafilter on the table just like real barista please the sound is so satisfying

Also please fix the game freezes when I exit it out and try to resume back into it

Also add pause button

Also add customer sounds like slurping and blowing when drinking the coffee

The tutorial for the game is perfect, it explains it well enough for you to know what to do and not feel completely lost as soon as you start the game. Everything just feels right about the game after that - it’s a perfect mix of pressure and fun. 

So far I have nothing bad to say about the game, but if the developer reads this I have a few things I think would be really cool to see in the game:

- Being able to customize your café, such as the name, colors, different furniture etc.

- Being able to add custom names to drinks (just for fun)

- Character customization, maybe custom outfits for a “work uniform”

- Multiplayer could be fun, being able to work in a café together and being able to visit other people’s cafés to hang out would be amazing

- Possibly upgraded graphics. The game looks really good currently, but maybe some more detailed textures would help the game be more immersive.

This game has so much potential, it’s really great and I can’t wait for any potential future updates. Sorry if anything I suggested is already in the game, I’m still quite new but I really hope this game is continuously updated. It’s one of my favorite games on vr currently.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much! I'm glad you are enjoying the game and this is really great feedback! many of the points you mentioned are on my Todo list already! Please join my discord to stay tuned to new updates https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh

reviews.review_title: Step Into the Life of a Barista
reviews.review_description:
Coffee Quest VR takes the popular VR cooking genre and gives it a realistic twist—putting you in the shoes of a true barista. You’ll need to follow strict recipes, juggle multiple drink and food orders, and keep up with waves of impatient customers. It’s stressful, but in the best possible way.

Beyond drinks, the game adds depth with tasks like maintaining cleanliness and even providing entertainment for your café. It feels like a genuine glimpse into the challenges of running a coffee shop.

It would be exciting to see an expansion into the financial side—managing profits, making business decisions, and growing your café. For now, it’s already a fun and immersive experience, perfect for short sessions or for progressing step by step into becoming the best VR barista. Well done!

developer_response.body: I really appreciate the detailed feedback. I love building systems that feel authentic, and your notes align with areas I’m exploring. If you’d like to follow the roadmap and weigh in on design choices, please join the Discord to stay updated and share more feedback: https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh

reviews.review_title: Please add measurements in the computer!
reviews.review_description: I love this game! I snatched it up as soon as it launched and have loved it ever since! It truly makes me feel like I get to run my own lil starbucks. Honestly I do encounter a few bugs sometimes when first loading in the game, mostly loading in to black space outside of the coffee shop and not being able to move which means I have to exit the game and load back in, but to me this isn't a huge issue. My one major gripe however is if you forget how to make a drink, even if it's just a single one, the only way to learn again is to  re-do the entirety of the tutorial! So this is my plea to the developer, I truly think there is already the perfect in game place to house a handy little guide spot, the in game computer! Maybe there could be a recipe/measurements button somewhere where it would help show the correct ratios for drinks in case you need a reminder in game. Just anything other than having to do the entire tutorial over again would be so incredibly helpful!

developer_response.body: I'm so happy your love the game! Thank you so much for the feedback! Please join our discord to chat more about the measurements issue. Regarding the tutorial issue, I'm aware of this bug, it's in one of the tutorials and forces you to repeat it again if you do a mistake. (it will be fixed soon) and regarding recipe measurements I'm more than happy to discuss your idea further on the discord channel https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh

reviews.review_description: the whole gameplay for this game is perfect. i love every part of this game but i only wish there was multiplayer. sometimes i just wanna play with my friends in a chill game and this game i feel like would be perfect for multiplayer

reviews.review_title: Love! This is the best coffee game!
reviews.review_description: Best coffee game out there. Runs smooth and the devs have worked hard to make a relaxed mode to practice in.  A frequent go-to in my library!

It's a surprisingly relaxing game. I didn't think the act of crafting my very own CUP 'O 'CHINO would feel so cozy and therapeutic but there's nothing I enjoy more than manual labor. There IS nothing better than producing a fresh order for the different customers in the store and it feels great to keep WATCHING your store grow and become more lively. 10/10 cured my depression and works great on my Quest 3

I have to say I'm happy I took a chance on this game and bought it. As I thought it would be, it's tough to manage everything with the needed speed and precision (I'll get to that), but my GOD is this hard to keep up with new customers every other minute as well as foods, dirt, dishes, etc! 😅
I'm incredibly thankful there is now a casual mode though, as for someone that spent the last 11 years recovering from a near death traumatic brain injury, I'm ever so slightly too slow and have to keep triple checking who wants what and what goes where while 3 MORE customers have walked in... 😂
So even Casual mode stresses me a bit eventually, but it's also easy to close the shop when getting overwhelmed as well. 
So I still enjoy my time with it and is very well done, but grabbing things can be an issue at times with so many items being close together. When you're trying to be quick, grabbing 3 cups or a pen while trying to grab a to-go lid can be a touch annoying. As well as grabbing the plats when trying to pick up trays. It's definitely not an issue that hinders the game much either, just something to be aware of in the heat of fulfilling 3 different orders. 😅

One thing I would love to see though is more to work towards in game. Working for ways to decorate/ change your entire shop if not even expanding it a bit. As right now there basically only seems to be recipes, ingredients, and appliances to make the job easier to work towards. Not bad at all considering this is just a casual game, but as I stated; it's just something I'd love to see. 🙂


Definitely recommend this game to anyone who enjoys these types of "job simulator" VR games. This is amongst the best of them!

reviews.review_description:
Fun game and really neat concept for an AppLab purchase.

I really like that you can control the flow of customers by Opening and Closing the shop at will. It allows you to purchase new items, and recipes etc while tidying up for the next rush. 

Some things I ran into while playing that may need improving and an idea to add as a feature:

1. Smooth turning. I like the free movement option, but the snap turn takes away from the immersion. A smooth turn option with turning speed inclusion would make it perfect.  

2. There’s some jankyness with the vacuum. When finished using it, and trying to return it to the wall mount..it doesn’t go back in the original spot on the wall…it kind of just glitches outwards and only resets when you do other things within the game or Open/Close the shop.

3. The drawer where you have the plates and trays…I really like the way you included a number of plates so I know I need to buy more etc. and that when you take a plate out of the drawer, and then return it, the plate automatically returns to the spot or pile of plates. Well…the trays and boxes do not do that. Hoping you can add the same feature you have for the plates, to the trays and boxes. I do like that the trays and boxes seem to be infinite, but if you accidentally pick one up and try to return it…it just sits on top of the main box or tray to initiate the pick up of. 


I have a video of the vacuum jank and the drawer issue I ran into if needed. 

Game is a lot of fun and I look forward to more updates with maybe new scenery or customization options!

Edit: thank you to the devs for a swift reply. Look forward to helping out on the discord and getting some nice updates in the future!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the positive review! the smooth turning suggestion is great and is on my todo list. I'm currently working on adding Casual Mode first (a mode without restrictions or time limit, but also no reward),  After that we'll look into releasing smooth turning, I'll also take a look at the 2 issues you mentioned. For now, you can toss the extra trays/boxes in the trash, and I'll into why they're not snapping back. And the vacuum is configured to snap back after 10 seconds to the original pose, but I'll look into why it's janky on release.

Please consider joining our discord (https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh) to share any new suggestions you have, and keep track on the progress for the next release

reviews.review_description: Running a Coffee shop is stressful. Its a fun game where you start making simple drinks but more customers you sevre the more drinks you can unlock. It has the full barista experience and the mechanics work really well its a fun game. There are plenty of bugs in the game but once you get past those can keep you busy for a while.

reviews.review_title: It's a bit of an UNREALISTIC game🤔
reviews.review_description: This game is a little bit unrealistic. Who do you know runs a shop by themselves only to fix the coffee, clean the floors, wash the dishes and take out the trash, cook etc. everything without any kind of help! 🤔🤨I would have quit a long time ago if I had to do all of that by myself without any help. 👈🥺I hope they implement a casual mode without a rush. They should have an easy, medium, hard, and / or extreme mode. It's not a fun experience. I will revisit this game when the next update comes out.

I really like the game. It's like overcooked, but with a focus on making different coffee drinks like espresso, americano, cappuccino, etc. 

I particularly enjoy making fancy drinks and writing a name on it. Feels like I'm a starbucks barista!

Besides making coffee, you can also cook food for customers and clean the coffee shop. 

Overall, it's a fun casual game to play.

I love the game and I have completed all the training but when opening my shop it tells me I can’t buy the drinks because it’s locked in demo, however I brought the game from the store so not quite sure what had gone wrong. Hope it gets fixed as  I’ve really enjoyed playing it
This makes me SO happy as someone who loves coffee and grew up on time management games!!!! I can’t wait to see what’s next!

developer_response.body: I'm so happy you like the game! As we continue making the game better, please send us any suggestions or feedback on our Email or Discord :)

reviews.review_title: One of the best developed games!
reviews.review_description: I love this game! I enjoy the concept of opening and closing the coffee shop instead of doing levels! Only thing is I wish the orders were in order from first received to most recent received.. when I go through a rush I get so overwhelmed because the orders aren’t in order and I have to see who came first and which one I was working on at the moment. I lost a lot of customers because of that :( also I would love more upgrades like increases the customers patience (I know there is already one but I feel like they get inpatient so fast and their order takes a long time to complete). An option to buy more coffee cups like you can with the plates would be nice. Also if the orders could also be displayed above the coffee machine it would be easier! lol BUT overall love love love this game. Definitely worth the buy!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the amazing feedback! and I'm so happy you're enjoying the game! Also, I just pushed a fix for the order re-sorting issue now :) Thanks for catching that! :)

reviews.review_description: It would be great to have prompts next to the orders showing the steps for each type of coffee, instead of relying on players to memorize them. Just like the recently released game, *The Mixologist*.Cleaning up in the game isn’t very enjoyable. It would be nice to hire AI to handle these unnecessary tasks.

developer_response.body:
Hi, Thanks a lot for your positive feedback! I'm currently working on finishing up a new Casual Mode, which won't have time limits on orders and more freedom to customize your experience e.g. Disable floor and plates cleaning, etc.. 

As for the recipes, please join our discord (https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh) so we can discuss your suggestions to make them easier. The game has currently only 3 distinctive ways to make drinks which are covered by a tutorial for each that is required before unlocking the corresponding recipes. and the order screen shows all the ingredients and sizes for each one, so you just need to memorize which of the 3 categories this drink is based on whether it's a basic one, milk drink or powder based drink.

reviews.review_description: It is a great  simulation But i miss the option to have employees     I hope  this wel be come in the game.  I must have sad customers

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the positive review! We're glad you're enjoying the game. Adding employees (a robot AI that helps with serving orders and a robot floor vacuum) are on the list of things we'll add over time to the game! stay tuned for the next update, and feel free to join our discord to send more suggestions like this :) https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh

reviews.review_description: It runs smooth and very fun to play. Me and my kids really like it

developer_response.body: What's up CalebVR.12! 👋 Thanks so much for spreading the love! We're so happy my game is hitting the spot for you. Stay tuned for more! Join our Discord at https://discord.com/invite/Zdf5zRXbnh for updates and to connect with other players! Cheers! ☕
